novel_features.name: One-shot booking
novel_features.description: Schedule an attendee onto one of your event types in a single composed call — slot check, optional reservation, create, optional confirm. For the rare scripting case (admin onboarding, recruiter pre-fill, test fixtures); for the normal flow where the attendee picks their own time, share a URL from link list.
novel_features.rationale: Cal.com forces a 4-call dance to create a booking; we compose the chain transactionally with --dry-run support. Bearer-token auth means the caller is the host, so this fits the rare host-scripts-an-attendee case — not the normal attendee-picks-time-from-link flow.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when you (the host) want to script a booking on the attendee's behalf rather than having them click your booking link. For the normal flow, share a `link list` URL.

novel_features.name: Unified agenda
novel_features.description: Upcoming bookings in a window — today, this week, or any duration — read from the local store.
novel_features.rationale: Joins bookings + event_types + attendees from local SQLite; no API call after sync, so agents stay cheap on context.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this whenever an agent needs 'what's on my calendar'; single command across any time window.

novel_features.name: Cross-event-type slot search
novel_features.description: Find first available slots across multiple event-type IDs in one call, ranked by start time.
novel_features.rationale: Cal.com's /v2/slots accepts one event-type per call; we fan out, merge, dedupe, and rank.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when you don't know which event type fits — let the caller pick from a ranked merged list.

novel_features.name: Booking analytics
novel_features.description: No-show, cancellation, volume, and density metrics over a window. Sister subcommands under analytics: bookings (volume), cancellations, no-show, density. --by accepts event-type, attendee, or weekday on the rate commands; analytics density --unit hour adds hourly heatmaps.
novel_features.rationale: Cal.com Insights is paid-tier; we compute the same metrics from the local store via SQL aggregation.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for capacity planning, no-show trend analysis, or attendee follow-up — answers no single API call provides.

novel_features.name: Conflict detection
novel_features.description: Detects overlapping bookings within a time window — pairs whose time ranges intersect get reported. Reads the local store, no API call.
novel_features.rationale: Cal.com normally prevents overlap on the same event type; this catches cross-event-type collisions, after-the-fact reassignments, and timezone-mismatch artifacts the API doesn't surface.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Run before sending confirmations or after a bulk reschedule — surfaces double-bookings the API silently allows.

novel_features.name: Availability gap finder
novel_features.description: Finds open windows in your schedule that are available but unbooked, filtered by minimum block size.
novel_features.rationale: Joins local schedules (availability windows) with bookings to compute open intervals; no API filter exists.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for capacity planning — answers 'when can I take a meeting' rather than 'what's on my plate'.

novel_features.name: Team workload balance
novel_features.description: Booking distribution across team members over a window — surfaces overloaded vs underutilized hosts.
novel_features.rationale: Live /v2/teams/{id}/bookings + per-host aggregation; no team-workload endpoint exists in Cal.com.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for round-robin tuning or to spot host burnout before it shows up as no-shows.

novel_features.name: Webhook coverage
novel_features.description: Audits registered webhook triggers against the canonical set and reports lifecycle events with no subscriber.
novel_features.rationale: Compares /v2/webhooks triggers vs an inlined canonical-trigger constant table; no vendor tool ships this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Run this whenever you add a new automation — surfaces missed triggers like BOOKING_NO_SHOW_UPDATED before they bite.

novel_features.name: Stale event types
novel_features.description: Event types with zero bookings in the last N days — candidates for removal.
novel_features.rationale: Left-joins local event_types against bookings filtered by --days; no analogous API filter exists.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for quarterly cleanup — keeps your bookable surface from drifting.

novel_features.name: Reschedule to next open slot
novel_features.description: Move an existing booking to the next available slot for the same event type, after a cutoff.
novel_features.rationale: Composes /v2/bookings/{uid} + /v2/slots + /v2/bookings/{uid}/reschedule; no prior tool composes this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for last-minute bumps — one command instead of three, with dry-run safety.

novel_features.name: Bookable link create
novel_features.description: Create a new bookable link (event type) on your Cal.com account; prints the cal.com/<your-username>/<slug> URL ready to share.
novel_features.rationale: Creating bookable links is the host's primary creative act on Cal.com. Wraps POST /v2/event-types with sensible defaults (auto-derived title) and renders the bookable URL via /v2/me lookup so the host can copy-share without composing the URL by hand.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this whenever you need a new bookable URL — it's the host's primary creative act and the natural answer to 'how do I create a booking link'.

novel_features.name: Bookable link list
novel_features.description: List every bookable link you own with the full cal.com/<your-username>/<slug> URL pre-rendered for copy-share.
novel_features.rationale: GET /v2/event-types returns event-type-groups but not the bookable URL; we resolve the username via /v2/me and synthesize the URL on each entry so users don't have to compose it.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to see what links you've published and grab their URLs without hand-composing cal.com/<user>/<slug>.

novel_features.name: Out-of-office set
novel_features.description: Mark yourself out-of-office for a date range so Cal.com excludes the period from slot search; supports redirect-to-teammate for round-robin event types.
novel_features.rationale: Wraps POST /v2/me/ooo with natural-language time parsing ('today', 'tomorrow') and reason enum validation. Endpoint mirror exposes this as 'me user-ooocontroller-create-my-ooo' which is unergonomic.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Going on vacation? Sick? Run this once and stop getting booked — primary host scheduling-control workflow.

novel_features.name: Out-of-office list
novel_features.description: List your active and upcoming out-of-office entries, sorted by start time.
novel_features.rationale: Wraps GET /v2/me/ooo with the verbose endpoint-mirror command 'me user-ooocontroller-get-my-ooo' collapsed into a clean ooo-prefixed call.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Confirm your OOO entries are recorded before you head out — and find the IDs you'd need to cancel one with 'ooo delete'.

narrative.headline: Every Cal.com feature, plus offline agendas, composed booking flows, and analytics no other Cal.com tool ships.
narrative.value_prop: cal-com-pp-cli wraps the entire Cal.com v2 API and adds a local SQLite store of your bookings, event types, schedules, and team data. Composed intents like `book` and `reschedule next` collapse multi-call dances into one transactional command. Local-store analytics, conflict detection, and team workload land in milliseconds with no API call.
narrative.auth_narrative: Cal.com uses bearer tokens prefixed with `cal_live_` (live) or `cal_test_` (test). Set `CAL_COM_TOKEN` in your environment, or run `auth set-token` once. The CLI also accepts managed-user access tokens and OAuth access tokens through the same Authorization header. Per-resource API-version pinning via `cal-api-version` is handled automatically by the client.

quickstart.comment: Confirm CAL_COM_TOKEN is loaded; use auth set-token <token> to save one to the config
Pull bookings, event types, schedules, teams, and webhooks into the local store
See today's bookings without an API call
Fan out the slot search across event-type IDs
Compose slot check + reservation + create + confirm in one call

troubleshoots.fix: Re-run `auth set-token` with a `cal_live_` or `cal_test_` key from https://app.cal.com/settings/developer/api-keys

troubleshoots.symptom: sync reports 0 bookings but you have bookings on Cal.com
troubleshoots.fix: Cal.com's /v2/bookings defaults to upcoming only; pass `--include-past` or use a wider date range

troubleshoots.symptom: slots find returns nothing for a known-bookable event type
troubleshoots.fix: Slots respect the schedule's working hours and timezone — pass `--timezone America/Los_Angeles` (or your account TZ) explicitly
Slot reservations expire after 5 minutes; re-run with a fresh `--after` window

troubleshoots.symptom: agenda shows nothing after a fresh sync
troubleshoots.fix: agenda reads from the local store; ensure sync completed (`sync --json | jq '.synced_resources'`) and pass `--window 7d` to widen the lens

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for cal-com-pp-cli whenever an agent needs to read or mutate a Cal.com calendar without burning context on the multi-call dance the API requires for booking and rescheduling. The local store makes 'what's on my calendar', 'when am I free', 'who is overloaded', and 'where are my conflicts' near-instant offline answers. The composed `book` and `reschedule next` commands are the right tools for transactional bookings; the endpoint-mirror coverage is there for everything else.

recipes.title: Create a bookable link and grab its URL
recipes.explanation: The host's primary creative act. Returns the bookable cal.com/<username>/<slug> URL pre-rendered.

recipes.title: Mark yourself out-of-office
recipes.explanation: While the OOO entry is active, Cal.com excludes the period from slot search so you don't get booked.

recipes.title: Today's agenda from the local store
recipes.explanation: Returns just the four fields an agent needs from the agenda envelope — keeps context tight against deeply-nested booking payloads.

recipes.title: Cross-event-type slot search ranked by start
recipes.explanation: Fans out /v2/slots per event-type ID; returns only the earliest slot.

recipes.title: No-show rate by attendee for capacity planning
recipes.explanation: Local SQL aggregation over synced bookings; no API call.

recipes.title: Audit webhook coverage before adding automation
recipes.explanation: Compares your registered triggers against the canonical Cal.com lifecycle set and surfaces missing subscribers.

recipes.title: Reschedule a booking to the next free slot
recipes.explanation: One composed command replaces three; --dry-run prints the planned move without committing.

trigger_phrases: create a booking link on cal.com
mark me out of office on cal.com
